## Summary

<!-- One or two sentences: what does this PR do and why? -->

## Related issue

<!-- Link the issue this addresses. PRs for substantial features should have a prior issue for scope discussion. -->
Fixes #<issue-number>

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that changes existing API)
- [ ] Documentation update (README, DocC articles, doc comments)
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Refactor (no behavior change)

## What changed

<!-- Bullet list of notable changes. Keep it scannable. -->

## Testing

<!-- How did you verify this works? -->
- [ ] Added or updated unit tests
- [ ] Added or updated snapshot tests (if rendering output changed)
- [ ] Ran `swift test` locally
- [ ] Ran the example app (if UI-facing)
- [ ] Benchmarked (if touching the hot path — see [Docs/Performance.md](Docs/Performance.md))

## Checklist

- [ ] Updated [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) under `## Unreleased` (or extended an existing entry)
- [ ] Updated DocC articles or doc comments if public API changed
- [ ] Updated README if user-facing behavior changed
- [ ] This change respects Quill's [Non-Goals](README.md#non-goals)
- [ ] I have read [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)
